insight: The AFK warning in /tell (lang key command-tell-afk-warning, PrivateMessage.java) is rendered with Variables.builder(sender.getAudience()), so any PAPI placeholder a user adds to it resolves against the message SENDER, not the AFK receiver. Receiver-context PAPI placeholders (e.g. %essentials_afk_reason%) return the sender's value locally and can never work for cross-server receivers, who have no local Player object. Receiver data in PMs must come from NetworkDirectory.Entry fields, not PAPI.

Per-player state shown cross-server (AFK, vanish, nick) must live as a field on NetworkDirectory.Entry wired into toMap/fromMap/applyPatch and be populated in ProxyChat.buildEntryMap, since the proxy re-serializes entries via Entry.toMap() and drops anything else. Fields that reset on server switch (like Essentials AFK state) must always be emitted in Entry.toMap(), or other backends keep stale values after DATABASE_READY.
